entries.title: Toga Pulls Deku Off The Planned Battlefield

context.label: Toga pulls Deku to Okuto Island

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.

content.fact: Toga’s sudden pull disrupts the hero separation plan by dragging Deku to the wrong battlefield.
content.injection: Use Toga’s interference as a serious tactical delay; Deku being absent from Floating U.A. has consequences.

content.fact: Toga asks for recognition in the language of love while still threatening and harming others.
content.injection: Write Toga’s emotions as sincere but dangerous; do not sanitize her violence or deny her emotional need.

entries.title: Deku Cannot Answer Toga The Way Ochaco Can

context.label: Toga pulls Deku to Okuto Island

content.fact: Deku can reject Toga’s violence but cannot fully meet the emotional question that Ochaco later addresses.
content.injection: Let Deku be compassionate but mismatched here; this conflict belongs primarily to Ochaco and Toga.

entries.title: Twice Blood Turns Toga Into Mass-Casualty Threat

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Toga’s access to Twice’s blood allows her to unleash a version of Sad Man’s Parade with enormous battlefield danger.
content.injection: After this anchor, treat Toga as a mass-threat through Twice duplication, not only a knife fighter.

entries.title: Sad Man’s Parade Strains Every Hero Line

content.fact: The clone swarm threatens to overwhelm hero coordination across battlefields.
content.injection: Portray the clone crisis as exponential pressure; ordinary crowd control must not solve it easily.

entries.title: Ochaco Chooses To Understand Without Excusing

context.label: Ochaco reaches Toga during clone crisis

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Ochaco tries to understand Toga’s pain and smile without excusing the harm Toga caused.
content.injection: Keep Ochaco’s compassion morally bounded; she reaches for Toga without pretending the victims do not matter.

entries.title: Zero Gravity Awakening Expands Rescue Scope

context.label: Ochaco reaches Toga during clone crisis

content.fact: Ochaco’s Quirk expansion lets her affect the battlefield at a scale tied to saving people rather than defeating Toga by force.
content.injection: Use Ochaco’s Quirk awakening as rescue-driven escalation; it should reflect her heroic motive, not just power creep.

entries.title: Toga Fate Should Remain Ambiguous Until Epilogue Context

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Toga’s final condition is tied to late-war and epilogue grief rather than immediate battlefield certainty for every character.
content.injection: Do not let uninvolved characters know Toga’s final condition instantly; use grief and uncertainty where appropriate.

entries.title: Ochaco Carries Toga Grief Into Postwar Purpose

content.fact: Ochaco’s grief over Toga helps shape her later commitment to helping children with difficult Quirks and emotions.
